---
title: Service Communication
description: Call functions across services with reliability guarantees
---

Service communication helpers enable reliable function calls between services with automatic retries, exactly-once delivery, and durable execution guarantees.

## ctx.serviceCall()

Make request-response calls to other services. Your function waits for the result before continuing.

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
// Call Basic Service function
const validation = await ctx.serviceCall(ValidationService).validateOrder(order);

// Call with error handling
try {
  const result = await ctx.serviceCall(PaymentService).processPayment(payment);
  return { success: true, transactionId: result.id };
} catch (error) {
  return { success: false, error: error.message };
}
```

```java Java
// Call Basic Service function
ValidationResult validation = ValidationServiceClient.fromContext(ctx)
    .validateOrder(order).await();

// Call with error handling
try {
    PaymentResult result = PaymentServiceClient.fromContext(ctx)
        .processPayment(payment).await();
    return new ProcessResult(true, result.getId());
} catch (Exception error) {
    return new ProcessResult(false, error.getMessage());
}
```

```python Python
# Call Basic Service function
validation = await ValidationServiceClient.from_context(ctx).validate_order(order)

# Call with error handling
try:
    result = await PaymentServiceClient.from_context(ctx).process_payment(payment)
    return {"success": True, "transaction_id": result.id}
except Exception as error:
    return {"success": False, "error": str(error)}
```
</CodeGroup>

Use request-response calls when you need the result immediately to continue processing.

## ctx.objectCall()

Call Virtual Object functions with a specific object key.

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
// Get user profile
const profile = await ctx.objectCall(UserAccount, userId).getProfile();

// Update user balance
const balanceResult = await ctx.objectCall(UserAccount, userId).updateBalance(amount);

// Call different users
const user1Profile = await ctx.objectCall(UserAccount, "user1").getProfile();
const user2Profile = await ctx.objectCall(UserAccount, "user2").getProfile();
```

```java Java
// Get user profile
ProfileResult profile = UserAccountClient.fromContext(ctx, userId)
    .getProfile().await();

// Update user balance
BalanceResult balanceResult = UserAccountClient.fromContext(ctx, userId)
    .updateBalance(amount).await();
```

```python Python
# Get user profile
profile = await UserAccountClient.from_context(ctx, user_id).get_profile()

# Update user balance
balance_result = await UserAccountClient.from_context(ctx, user_id).update_balance(amount)
```
</CodeGroup>

Each object key is processed independently, so calls to different keys can run in parallel.

## ctx.workflowCall()

Call Workflow functions to start workflows or query their status.

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
// Start a workflow
const workflowResult = await ctx.workflowCall(OrderWorkflow, orderId).processOrder(order);

// Query workflow status
const status = await ctx.workflowCall(OrderWorkflow, orderId).getStatus();

// Get workflow progress
const progress = await ctx.workflowCall(OrderWorkflow, orderId).getProgress();
```

```java Java
// Start a workflow
OrderResult workflowResult = OrderWorkflowClient.fromContext(ctx, orderId)
    .processOrder(order).await();

// Query workflow status
StatusResult status = OrderWorkflowClient.fromContext(ctx, orderId)
    .getStatus().await();
```

```python Python
# Start a workflow
workflow_result = await OrderWorkflowClient.from_context(ctx, order_id).process_order(order)

# Query workflow status
status = await OrderWorkflowClient.from_context(ctx, order_id).get_status()
```
</CodeGroup>

## ctx.serviceSend()

Make one-way calls that don't return results. Your function continues immediately without waiting.

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
// Fire-and-forget notification
ctx.serviceSend(NotificationService).sendEmail(userId, "Welcome!");

// Background analytics
ctx.serviceSend(AnalyticsService).recordEvent("user_signup", { 
  userId, 
  timestamp: new Date().toISOString() 
});

// Cleanup task
ctx.serviceSend(CleanupService).deleteExpiredData(userId);
```

```java Java
// Fire-and-forget calls
NotificationServiceClient.fromContext(ctx).send()
    .sendEmail(userId, "Welcome!");

AnalyticsServiceClient.fromContext(ctx).send()
    .recordEvent("user_signup", eventData);

CleanupServiceClient.fromContext(ctx).send()
    .deleteExpiredData(userId);
```

```python Python
# Fire-and-forget calls
NotificationServiceClient.from_context(ctx).send().send_email(user_id, "Welcome!")

AnalyticsServiceClient.from_context(ctx).send().record_event("user_signup", event_data)

CleanupServiceClient.from_context(ctx).send().delete_expired_data(user_id)
```
</CodeGroup>

Use one-way calls for background tasks where you don't need the result.

## ctx.objectSend()

Send one-way calls to Virtual Object functions.

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
// Update multiple user accounts
userIds.forEach(userId => {
  ctx.objectSend(UserAccount, userId).updateLastSeen(new Date().toISOString());
});

// Notify all followers
followerIds.forEach(followerId => {
  ctx.objectSend(UserAccount, followerId).addNotification({
    type: "new_post",
    fromUserId: authorId,
    postId: post.id
  });
});
```
</CodeGroup>

## ctx.workflowSend()

Send signals to running workflows.

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
// Send payment confirmation to workflow
ctx.workflowSend(OrderWorkflow, orderId).confirmPayment({
  success: true,
  transactionId: "txn-123",
  amount: 99.99
});

// Cancel a workflow
ctx.workflowSend(OrderWorkflow, orderId).cancelOrder("Customer requested cancellation");

// Approve a workflow
ctx.workflowSend(ApprovalWorkflow, requestId).approve(true);
```

```java Java
// Send signals to workflows
OrderWorkflowClient.fromContext(ctx, orderId).send()
    .confirmPayment(paymentResult);

ApprovalWorkflowClient.fromContext(ctx, requestId).send()
    .approve(true);
```

```python Python
# Send signals to workflows
OrderWorkflowClient.from_context(ctx, order_id).send().confirm_payment(payment_result)

ApprovalWorkflowClient.from_context(ctx, request_id).send().approve(True)
```
</CodeGroup>

## Delayed calls

Schedule function calls to happen in the future.

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
// Schedule reminder for tomorrow
ctx.serviceCall(ReminderService).sendReminder(userId, message, {
  delay: 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000 // 24 hours
});

// Schedule timeout check
ctx.objectCall(UserAccount, userId).expireSession({
  delay: 30 * 60 * 1000 // 30 minutes
});

// Schedule workflow cancellation
ctx.workflowSend(OrderWorkflow, orderId).cancelIfNotPaid({
  delay: 15 * 60 * 1000 // 15 minutes
});
```

```java Java
// Schedule calls with delays
ReminderServiceClient.fromContext(ctx)
    .sendReminder(userId, message)
    .delayedCall(Duration.ofDays(1));

UserAccountClient.fromContext(ctx, userId)
    .expireSession()
    .delayedCall(Duration.ofMinutes(30));
```

```python Python
# Schedule calls with delays
ReminderServiceClient.from_context(ctx).send_reminder(user_id, message).delayed_call(
    timedelta(days=1)
)

UserAccountClient.from_context(ctx, user_id).expire_session().delayed_call(
    timedelta(minutes=30)
)
```
</CodeGroup>

## Error handling and retries

Service communication helpers automatically handle retries for transient failures:

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
export const processWithFallback = async (ctx: Context, data: ProcessData) => {
  try {
    // Try primary service
    return await ctx.serviceCall(PrimaryService).process(data);
  } catch (error) {
    // Fallback to secondary service
    try {
      return await ctx.serviceCall(SecondaryService).process(data);
    } catch (fallbackError) {
      // Both services failed
      throw new Error("All processing services unavailable");
    }
  }
};
```
</CodeGroup>

**Automatic retries**: Transient failures are automatically retried by Restate.

**Terminal errors**: Use `TerminalError` to stop retries for permanent failures.

**Exactly-once delivery**: Each service call is executed exactly once, even during retries.

Service communication helpers provide the foundation for building reliable, distributed applications where services can depend on each other without worrying about network failures or partial executions.
